deep_dive.title: STEB: A Speech-to-Speech Translation Expressiveness Benchmark for Evaluating Beyond Translation Fidelity
deep_dive.abstract: Speech-to-speech translation (S2ST) should preserve not only lexical meaning, but also expressive attributes: emotion, scenario style (e.g., news reporting vs. dramatic dialogue), and nonverbal vocalizations (NVs). Moreover, collecting cross-lingual target speech that is both translation-faithful and expressively aligned with the source is difficult at scale, making reference-based evaluation impractical. We introduce STEB (Speech-to-Speech Translation Expressiveness Benchmark), a 32.6-hour Chinese--English benchmark that evaluates both standard dimensions (translation fidelity, speaker similarity, duration alignment) and expressiveness dimensions (emotion, scenario style, NV preservation). For expressiveness evaluation, STEB uses a caption-then-summarize framework that converts speech into structured expressive attributes and compares source and hypothesis attributes with an LLM judge. Human validation shows statistically significant correlations with listener judgments across all expressive dimensions. We evaluate six S2ST systems covering cascaded systems, end-to-end models, and speech large language models. Many systems, especially cascaded ones, achieve strong translation fidelity, but they still struggle with emotion preservation (best: 3.82/5) and NV preservation (best: 2.31/5). These results reveal a gap between semantic transfer and expressive transfer, identifying expressiveness preservation as an open challenge for S2ST. Audio samples are available at https://cmots.github.io/steb.github.io/.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 语音到语音翻译的表现力基准
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 语音到语音翻译的表现力基准
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 语音到语音翻译的表现力基准
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: STEB is a 32.6-hour Chinese-English speech-to-speech translation benchmark evaluating translation fidelity, speaker similarity, duration alignment, emotion, scenario style, and nonverbal vocalization preservation with automatic metrics.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 现有语音翻译评测主要关注语义保真度，忽略了情感、场景风格和非语言声音等表现力维度，且跨语言目标数据难以大规模收集。本文提出STEB，一个32.6小时的中英语音翻译表现力基准，不仅评测翻译忠实度、说话人相似度和时长对齐，还引入情感、场景风格和非语言声音保留等表现力维度，并设计了自动评估指标。实验证明该基准能全面捕捉翻译系统中的表现力缺失，推动更自然的语音翻译发展。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 现有语音翻译评测主要关注语义保真度，忽略了情感、场景风格和非语言声音等表现力维度，且跨语言目标数据难以大规模收集。本文提出STEB，一个32.6小时的中英语音翻译表现力基准，不仅评测翻译忠实度、说话人相似度和时长对齐，还引入情感、场景风格和非语言声音保留等表现力维度，并设计了自动评估指标。实验证明该基准能全面捕捉翻译系统中的表现力缺失，推动更自然的语音翻译发展。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: Papers central to 调研多语言语音生成、语音转文字或方言相关工作, especially work that connects or combines: multilingual speech synthesis models and techniques; automatic speech recognition for multiple languages; processing and recognition of dialectal speech; voice conversion across languages and dialects; cross-lingual text to speech synthesis; speech technologies for low resource languages; multilingual speech synthesis state of the art; dialect speech recognition techniques; cross lingual voice conversion methods.

deep_dive.title: Closing the Quality Gap in Low-Resource Text-to-Speech: LoRA Fine-Tuning of VoxCPM2 for Khmer and Korean
deep_dive.abstract: Large pretrained text-to-speech (TTS) models sound almost human for well-resourced languages, but much worse for languages that are rare in their training data. We study this quality gap for Khmer and Korean using VoxCPM2, a 2.4B-parameter, tokenizer-free TTS model that joins a MiniCPM-4 language-model backbone with a flow-matching diffusion decoder. We build one shared, language-tagged corpus of about 26 hours and adapt VoxCPM2 with a single Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA) adapter, trained on both languages at once and added to both the language model and the decoder. The adapter is zero-initialized, so training starts exactly at the original (zero-shot) model. In native-speaker listening tests, the Khmer Mean Opinion Score (MOS) rises from 3.85 to 4.23 with the best adapter (rank 64), a highly significant gain (paired Wilcoxon test, p<0.001), while training only 0.19 to 3.03 percent of the parameters. The automatic loss and the human ratings, however, disagree on the best rank: validation loss is lowest at rank 128, yet MOS peaks at rank 64. The same adapter brings no gain for Korean, a language the base model already handles well, and at a high rank it even degrades quality. Adaptation therefore helps mainly where the base model is genuinely weak.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Low-resource text-to-speech for Khmer and Korean using LoRA fine-tuning
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 使用LoRA微调VoxCPM2模型，提升高棉语和韩语的低资源语音合成质量
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 使用LoRA微调VoxCPM2模型，提升高棉语和韩语的低资源语音合成质量
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 使用LoRA微调VoxCPM2模型，提升高棉语和韩语的低资源语音合成质量
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Studies quality gap in low-resource TTS for Khmer and Korean using VoxCPM2, proposing a single LoRA adapter fine-tuned on both languages, achieving improved MOS over zero-shot baseline.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 本文针对高棉语和韩语两种低资源语言，研究了大型预训练TTS模型在低资源场景下的质量差距。使用VoxCPM2模型（2.4B参数，基于MiniCPM-4骨干和流匹配扩散解码器），构建了一个共享的语言标记语料库，并通过单个LoRA适配器同时对两种语言进行微调。LoRA适配器零初始化，从原始零样本起点开始训练。听话人测试结果表明，高棉语的MOS显著提升，验证了该方法在低资源TTS中的有效性。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 本文针对高棉语和韩语两种低资源语言，研究了大型预训练TTS模型在低资源场景下的质量差距。使用VoxCPM2模型（2.4B参数，基于MiniCPM-4骨干和流匹配扩散解码器），构建了一个共享的语言标记语料库，并通过单个LoRA适配器同时对两种语言进行微调。LoRA适配器零初始化，从原始零样本起点开始训练。听话人测试结果表明，高棉语的MOS显著提升，验证了该方法在低资源TTS中的有效性。

deep_dive.title: KARLA: Knowledge-base Augmented Retrieval for Language Models
deep_dive.abstract: We propose a new method that allows an LLM to automatically pull in factual knowledge from a knowledge base during token generation. This means that (1)~factual knowledge in the LLM output can be updated without retraining the LLM, (2)~facts in the LLM output can be traced to the knowledge base for transparency and explainability, and (3)~smaller models can achieve the same factual accuracy as larger models. Our core idea is to train the model to produce special tokens that trigger a query to the knowledge base. Our experiments show that our method improves factual grounding in both short and long-form generation, and allows factual revisions to take effect through KB edits rather than parameter updates.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Retrieval-augmented generation for factual knowledge grounding in LLMs
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 利用检索增强生成提升LLM事实知识准确性
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 利用检索增强生成提升LLM事实知识准确性
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 利用检索增强生成提升LLM事实知识准确性
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes KARLA, a method that trains LLMs to produce special tokens to query a knowledge base during generation, improving factual accuracy and enabling traceable updates without retraining.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 本文提出KARLA方法，使大语言模型在生成过程中自动查询知识库以获取事实知识。该方法训练模型产生特殊令牌触发检索，从而在不重新训练的情况下更新和追踪模型输出中的事实信息。实验表明，KARLA显著提升了长短文本生成的事实准确性，并允许通过编辑知识库而非参数更新来修正事实。该工作为将检索增强生成应用于事实性推理等任务提供了通用框架。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 本文提出KARLA方法，使大语言模型在生成过程中自动查询知识库以获取事实知识。该方法训练模型产生特殊令牌触发检索，从而在不重新训练的情况下更新和追踪模型输出中的事实信息。实验表明，KARLA显著提升了长短文本生成的事实准确性，并允许通过编辑知识库而非参数更新来修正事实。该工作为将检索增强生成应用于事实性推理等任务提供了通用框架。

deep_dive.title: CN-NewsTTS Bench: a target-level automatic benchmark for raw-input Chinese news TTS pronunciation
deep_dive.abstract: Chinese news text contains dense written forms such as scores, hyphenated model names, ranges, unit symbols, percentages, English abbreviations, and mixed Chinese-Latin-digit names. These forms are frequent in real listening workflows, and a text-to-speech (TTS) system can preserve the written string while changing the spoken meaning. We introduce CN-NewsTTS Bench v0.1, an open target-level benchmark for evaluating whether Chinese news TTS products pronounce such targets correctly from raw text, without user-side rules, LLM rewriting, SSML hints, or manual edits. The release contains a 200-record development set, an 800-record public test set, 992 public auto-evaluable targets, fixed transcripts from a three-ASR ensemble, an automatic target scorer, and initial results for seven product TTS systems. We additionally report ASR-route diagnostics, ASR-subset ablations, category-level results, confidence intervals, and provider configuration metadata. The best system reaches 0.879 strict accuracy, while several systems remain below 0.60.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: TTS pronunciation benchmark for Chinese news
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 中文新闻TTS发音基准测试
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 中文新闻TTS发音基准测试
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 中文新闻TTS发音基准测试
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: CN-NewsTTS Bench is a target-level automatic benchmark for evaluating Chinese news TTS pronunciation from raw text, covering scores, model names, and other dense written forms with a 1000-record set and auto-evaluation via ASR ensemble.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 现有TTS评测多依赖人工或规则，无法自动评估中文新闻文本中分数、型号等密集书写形式的发音正确性。本文提出CN-NewsTTS Bench，一个面向原始输入的自动基准测试，包含1000条测试记录和992个可自动评估的目标，通过三模型ASR集成获取固定转录并进行自动评测。实验表明该基准能有效区分不同TTS系统在复杂文本上的发音表现，为中文新闻TTS提供了标准化评测工具。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 现有TTS评测多依赖人工或规则，无法自动评估中文新闻文本中分数、型号等密集书写形式的发音正确性。本文提出CN-NewsTTS Bench，一个面向原始输入的自动基准测试，包含1000条测试记录和992个可自动评估的目标，通过三模型ASR集成获取固定转录并进行自动评测。实验表明该基准能有效区分不同TTS系统在复杂文本上的发音表现，为中文新闻TTS提供了标准化评测工具。

deep_dive.title: ZONOS2 Technical Report
deep_dive.abstract: We present ZONOS2 8B, our latest TTS model, which achieves state-of-the-art naturalness, prosody, and voice cloning fidelity. We improve upon Zonos-v0.1 across scale, data, and training recipe. We scale the model from 1.6B to 8B total parameters (900M active) with a novel mixture-of-experts (MoE) backbone, improving inference latency and throughput. We expand our training corpus from 200K to over 6M hours using a new data processing pipeline, and we simplify our post-training and conditioning recipes to improve naturalness and voice cloning fidelity. We evaluate ZONOS2 8B on quality, speaker similarity, WER, and ZTTS1-Eval, our novel TTS benchmark, where it performs competitively with state-of-the-art systems while maintaining good streaming latency. We release our model weights and example inference code under an Apache 2.0 license on GitHub and Hugging Face.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: state-of-the-art TTS model with MoE backbone, large-scale training, and novel benchmark
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 具有MoE骨干、大规模训练和新基准的最先进TTS模型
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 具有MoE骨干、大规模训练和新基准的最先进TTS模型
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 具有MoE骨干、大规模训练和新基准的最先进TTS模型
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Presents ZONOS2 8B, a TTS model with 8B parameters (900M active) using mixture-of-experts, trained on 6M hours, achieving competitive results on quality, speaker similarity, and a new TTS benchmark.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该技术报告介绍ZONOS2 8B，一个具有80亿参数（其中9亿激活）的最先进TTS模型，采用混合专家（MoE）骨干。训练数据从20万小时扩展到超过600万小时。模型在质量、说话人相似度、字错误率以及新的ZTTS1-Eval基准上表现具有竞争力。报告还开源了模型权重和推理示例，为TTS领域提供了强大的基线系统。

deep_dive.title: ParaPairAudioBench: Paralinguistic Pairwise Audio Benchmark for LALM-as-a-Judge
deep_dive.abstract: Large Audio-Language Models (LALMs) have been widely used as judge models for the automatic evaluation of generated speech. However, prior approaches predominantly focus on holistic naturalness, leaving fine-grained paralinguistic distinctions underexplored. We introduce ParaPairAudioBench, a pairwise benchmark of 5,175 audio pairs across five paralinguistic dimensions: Style, Rate, Emphasis, Age, and Gender. Our experiments show that current LALM judges still lag behind human judgments by 32%p on average and exhibit severe calibration failures, particularly in Tie cases where the correct decision is to abstain. To further analyze lexical versus acoustic reliance, the benchmark includes both same-transcript and cross-transcript conditions. ParaPairAudioBench enables multi-dimensional, calibration-aware assessment of the reliability of LALM-as-a-Judge for paralinguistic speech evaluation.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: paralinguistic pairwise audio benchmark for LALM-as-a-judge evaluation
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 面向LALM评判的副语言成对音频基准
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 面向LALM评判的副语言成对音频基准
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 面向LALM评判的副语言成对音频基准
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Introduces ParaPairAudioBench, a pairwise benchmark of 5,175 audio pairs across five paralinguistic dimensions (Style, Rate, Emphasis, Age, Gender) for evaluating LALM judges, showing a 32% gap to human judgments.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文提出了ParaPairAudioBench，一个包含5175个音频对的副语言成对基准，覆盖风格、语速、重音、年龄和性别五个维度，用于评估大型音频语言模型（LALM）作为评判者的能力。实验表明当前LALM评判平均落后人类32个百分点，尤其在平局情况下存在严重的校准失败。基准还包括同转录本和跨转录本条件，以分析词汇与声学依赖。该工作为自动化语音评估提供了细粒度的副语言评价标准。

quick_skim.title: Speaker Identity in Non-Verbal Vocalizations: Conditional Distillation and Mixture of Experts Approach
quick_skim.abstract: As expressive text-to-speech (TTS) and voice conversion (VC) systems increasingly generate non-verbal vocalizations (NVVs) to enhance naturalness, reliable speaker verification (SV) becomes essential to objectively assess identity consistency across both verbal and non-verbal segments. Yet current SV systems generalize poorly to NVVs, and fine-tuning on NVV data causes catastrophic forgetting of speech performance. We present the first systematic study across 10 NVV types and propose a framework combining frozen Data2Vec self-supervised features with ECAPA-TDNN, enhanced by a Mixture of Experts (MoE) module with learned domain-aware routing. A conditional distillation loss on speech inputs via a pretrained teacher retains speech-to-speech accuracy, while a contrastive loss bridges the speech-NVV domain gap. Our method reduces speech-NVV EER from 38.93% to 22.66% over a pretrained baseline, and improves speech EER from 13.17% to 9.24% via distillation.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: speaker verification for TTS and voice conversion across vocalization types
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 针对TTS和语音转换中的非语言发声进行说话人身份验证

quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 针对TTS和语音转换中的非语言发声进行说话人身份验证
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: First systematic study on speaker verification for non-verbal vocalizations in expressive TTS/VC, combining frozen Data2Vec features with ECAPA-TDNN and MoE.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 随着TTS和语音转换系统生成非语言发声以提升自然度，可靠地评估身份一致性成为挑战。该论文首次系统研究10种非语言发声类型的说话人验证，提出结合冻结的Data2Vec自监督特征与ECAPA-TDNN的框架，并引入混合专家路由和条件蒸馏损失，在保持言语识别性能的同时提升非语言发声验证准确率。

quick_skim.title: Phonemes to the Rescue: Multilingual Tokenization Based on International Phonetic Alphabet
quick_skim.abstract: Multilingual language models often exhibit performance disparities across languages that can arise as early as the tokenization stage. Widely-used subword tokenization approaches favor high-resource languages, and tokenizer-free methods still yield longer sequences for scripts with a higher bytes-per-character ratio. To address these shortcomings, we propose to use the International Phonetic Alphabet (IPA) as a language-agnostic input representation for multilingual tokenizers. IPA provides a compact symbol inventory, greater cross-lingual character overlap, and a more balanced byte-per-character distribution across languages. We train matched pairs of text vs. IPA subword tokenizers across 24 languages and 14 scripts and demonstrate that IPA tokenizers consistently improve tokenization quality, especially for non-Latin scripts, and generalize more effectively to unseen languages and scripts.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: multilingual tokenization using IPA benefits low-resource scripts
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 使用IPA进行多语言分词，利好低资源脚本
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 使用IPA进行多语言分词，利好低资源脚本
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 使用IPA进行多语言分词，利好低资源脚本
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes IPA-based tokenizer that reduces cross-lingual representation disparity, shown on 24 languages and 14 scripts.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 子词分词器偏向高资源语言，导致低资源脚本性能差异。本文提出基于国际音标（IPA）的语言无关分词表示，在24种语言和14种脚本上训练与文本分词器的配对比较。IPA分词器在压缩率和跨语言重叠上优于传统方法，为多语言模型提供更公平的输入表示，尤其利好低资源语言。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 子词分词器偏向高资源语言，导致低资源脚本性能差异。本文提出基于国际音标（IPA）的语言无关分词表示，在24种语言和14种脚本上训练与文本分词器的配对比较。IPA分词器在压缩率和跨语言重叠上优于传统方法，为多语言模型提供更公平的输入表示，尤其利好低资源语言。

quick_skim.title: Synthetic Audio Generation Framework for Air Traffic Control Speech Recognition
quick_skim.abstract: Automatic Speech Recognition (ASR) systems, despite achieving remarkable accuracy in general-purpose domains with native speech (L1), struggle in domains like Air Traffic Control (ATC) due to strong channel noise, a presence of non-native (L2) English accents, and data scarcity. We propose a synthetic data generation pipeline with acoustical properties simulations specifically designed to address this lack of real data to improve recognition accuracy in the ATC domain. Our approach leverages a combination of neural generation techniques, including Text-to-Speech, Voice Conversion, L2-to-L1 accent conversion, and a novel controllable L1-to-L2 accent conversion framework built to simulate accented speech. Our experiments with the Whisper model on the ATCO2 corpus demonstrate that fine-tuning with either synthetic data alone, or a mix of real and synthetic data, significantly improves the word error rate over out-of-the-box and real data only baselines respectively.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: synthetic audio generation framework for ASR in ATC domain with accent conversion
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 用于空中交通管制ASR的合成音频生成框架，包括口音转换
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 用于空中交通管制ASR的合成音频生成框架，包括口音转换
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 用于空中交通管制ASR的合成音频生成框架，包括口音转换
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes a synthetic data pipeline combining TTS, voice conversion, and accent conversion to improve ASR for ATC speech with non-native accents, showing gains with Whisper model.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对空中交通管制（ATC）领域自动语音识别（ASR）面临的强通道噪声、非母语口音和数据稀缺问题，提出一个合成音频生成流水线。该方法结合文本转语音、语音转换、L2到L1口音转换以及新颖的可控L1到L2口音转换框架来模拟带口音语音。在Whisper模型上的实验表明，该方法显著提升了识别准确率，为低资源领域ASR提供了有效的数据增强策略。

quick_skim.title: Integrating Facial Generation into Full-Duplex Spoken Dialogue Systems
quick_skim.abstract: Full-duplex spoken dialogue models, such as Moshi, enable natural, low-latency voice conversations. However, they remain limited to the audio modality, lacking the facial expressions that are integral to human communication. We present Moshi-Face, the first full-duplex dialogue model that jointly processes the user's audio and facial input while simultaneously generating speech and facial motion. We first construct a vector-quantized variational autoencoder (VQ-VAE) as a face codec that encodes 3D head meshes extracted from facial videos into compact discrete tokens, referred to as face tokens, and conversely reconstructs 3D meshes from these tokens. We then extend Moshi with a Face Transformer module that generates face tokens non-autoregressively, enabling Moshi-Face to produce synchronized audio and face tokens in real time. Experiments show that Moshi-Face achieves audiovisual alignment at low latency while preserving the dialogue quality of the original audio-only model.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: full-duplex spoken dialogue with facial generation
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 全双工语音对话结合面部生成
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 全双工语音对话结合面部生成
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 全双工语音对话结合面部生成
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Moshi-Face extends full-duplex spoken dialogue models with joint audio and facial input processing and simultaneous speech and facial motion generation using a VQ-VAE face codec and non-autoregressive Face Transformer.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 现有全双工语音对话模型如Moshi仅处理音频，缺乏面部表情这一人类交流的关键要素。本文提出Moshi-Face，首个联合处理用户音频和面部输入、同时生成语音和面部运动的全双工对话模型。通过VQ-VAE将3D头部网格编码为离散面部token，再使用非自回归Face Transformer生成面部运动。实验表明该方法在保持低延迟对话的同时生成合理的面部表情，拓展了语音交互的多模态能力。

quick_skim.title: CAT-Translate: Building Compact Open-Source Models for Japanese-English Translation
quick_skim.abstract: Nowadays, large multilingual translation models demonstrate impressive translation capabilities in the machine translation benchmarks. This raises a practical question to the developers: is it worth developing translation models specialized for a particular language pair if you only need to support that language pair? To give an anecdotal answer to this question, we develop a family of small language models (0.8B, 1.4B, 3.3B, and 7B parameters) specialized for Japanese-English bidirectional translation. We employ a two-stage supervised fine-tuning approach followed by Multi-Objective GRPO (Ichihara et al. 2025) to train models on synthetically generated parallel corpora. We evaluate our models on WMT and real-world translation benchmarks across business, legal, medical, financial, and patent domains. While multilingual models achieve strong performance on WMT benchmarks, our compact models outperform them on real-world benchmarks, suggesting the practical utility of developing specialized translation models even in the era of large multilingual models.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 面向日英双向翻译的紧凑型模型
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 面向日英双向翻译的紧凑型模型
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 面向日英双向翻译的紧凑型模型
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: CAT-Translate develops a family of small multilingual translation models (0.8B-7B) specialized for Japanese-English using two-stage SFT and Multi-Objective GRPO, achieving competitive performance on WMT benchmarks.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 大型多语言翻译模型在通用基准上表现出色，但对特定语言对是否需专门开发模型仍存疑问。本文针对日英双向翻译开发了0.8B至7B参数规模的紧凑模型家族，采用两阶段监督微调结合多目标GRPO训练方法，在合成平行语料上训练。实验表明这些专用模型在WMT及真实场景翻译基准上达到与大型模型竞争的性能，证明针对特定语言对开发紧凑模型的实用性。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 大型多语言翻译模型在通用基准上表现出色，但对特定语言对是否需专门开发模型仍存疑问。本文针对日英双向翻译开发了0.8B至7B参数规模的紧凑模型家族，采用两阶段监督微调结合多目标GRPO训练方法，在合成平行语料上训练。实验表明这些专用模型在WMT及真实场景翻译基准上达到与大型模型竞争的性能，证明针对特定语言对开发紧凑模型的实用性。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: Papers central to 跨语言翻译研究，特别是大模型对不可译词的字面化或非字面化翻译倾向, especially work that connects or combines: untranslatable words in cross-lingual translation; tendency of large language models towards literal translation; non-literal translation preferences in LLMs; bias towards literal or non-literal translation in multilingual models; cross-lingual transfer of semantic meaning; How do large language models handle untranslatable words in translation?; Do LLMs show a bias towards literal translation over non literal translation?; What factors influence the translation strategy of multilingual models for culturally specific terms?.

quick_skim.title: Only Ask What You Don't Know: Grounded Delta Planning for Efficient Multi-step RAG
quick_skim.abstract: Multi-hop question answering remains challenging for Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) because existing approaches either propagate errors across iterative retrieval rounds or over-generate reasoning steps, increasing cost without improving accuracy. We propose Grounded Delta Planning RAG (GDP-RAG), a plan-based framework that targets only the information delta based on three simple design choices: (1) preliminary retrieval to ground planning before execution, (2) a gap-conditioned planning prompt that asks only for missing information, and (3) a skeletal trajectory that pairs each subquery with a Thought capturing evidence from preliminary retrieval and carrying it through to the final answer. GDP-RAG focuses computation on unresolved gaps, yielding concise, reliable reasoning trajectories. Extensive experiments on HotpotQA, 2WikiMultiHopQA, and MuSiQue show that GDP-RAG achieves the highest accuracy (60.63%) among all compared systems while maintaining a cost-of-pass of 0.51, 22% lower than PAR-RAG (0.65) and 68% lower than KnowTrace (1.57), with no method achieving both higher accuracy and lower cost.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: retrieval augmented generation for multi-hop reasoning
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 检索增强生成用于多跳推理
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 检索增强生成用于多跳推理
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 检索增强生成用于多跳推理
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: GDP-RAG uses grounded delta planning to efficiently answer multi-hop questions by retrieving only missing information.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对多跳问答中RAG的误差传播和步骤冗余问题，提出基于接地增量规划的GDP-RAG框架。通过预检索锚定规划、缺口条件提示和骨架轨迹，仅针对缺失信息进行检索，减少了计算开销并提高了准确性。在多个数据集上优于现有RAG方法，为高效多步推理提供了新范式。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 该论文针对多跳问答中RAG的误差传播和步骤冗余问题，提出基于接地增量规划的GDP-RAG框架。通过预检索锚定规划、缺口条件提示和骨架轨迹，仅针对缺失信息进行检索，减少了计算开销并提高了准确性。在多个数据集上优于现有RAG方法，为高效多步推理提供了新范式。

quick_skim.title: Synthesizing the Lombard Effect: Multi-Level Control of Speech Clarity and Vocal Effort in TTS
quick_skim.abstract: Humans tend to speak louder and clearer in challenging environments, such as noisy conditions or when addressing hearingimpaired listeners, which is called Lombard effect. To simulate this behavior in speech synthesis systems, we introduce a flow-matching based text-to-speech (TTS) model trained with vocal effort and articulation pseudo-labels. The proposed model achieves continuous and disentangled control of vocal effort and articulation, while also enabling word-level emphasis for clarifying specific segments of an utterance. Experimental results show that these control mechanisms effectively improve clarityrelated acoustic features. Furthermore, speech-in-noise experiments demonstrate that our model successfully simulates the intelligibility gains of human clear speech in noisy conditions.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: controllable TTS with multi-level speech clarity and vocal effort
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 可控TTS，多层次语音清晰度和发声力度
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 可控TTS，多层次语音清晰度和发声力度
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 可控TTS，多层次语音清晰度和发声力度
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Introduces a flow-matching TTS model with continuous control of vocal effort and articulation, simulating the Lombard effect.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对语音合成中模拟真实环境中人们说话更清晰响亮的需求，本文提出基于流匹配的TTS模型，结合发声力度和发音伪标签，实现了对发声力度和发音的连续解耦控制，并支持词级强调。实验表明该模型有效改善了清晰度相关声学特征，并在噪声环境下成功模拟了Lombard效应。

quick_skim.title: Beyond U-Net: A Latent-Representation-Aligned Skip-Free Backbone for Flow-Matching Speech Enhancement
quick_skim.abstract: Generative models, particularly diffusion and score-based approaches, have recently achieved strong performance in speech enhancement, but their iterative sampling process limits real-time deployment. Flow Matching offers an efficient alternative by transporting noisy speech toward clean speech through an ordinary differential equation with few function evaluations. In this work, we propose a skip-free encoder-decoder backbone for flow-matching speech enhancement, guided by Latent Representation Alignment (LRA). Instead of relying on U-Net skip connections, which may transfer noise-correlated low-level features to the decoder, the proposed model aligns its bottleneck and decoder representations with clean latent features extracted from a frozen Descript Audio Codec encoder-decoder without quantization. This codec-aligned supervision promotes compact clean-speech representations while preserving efficient few-step inference. Experiments on WSJ0-CHiME3 and VoiceBank-DEMAND show improved PESQ and perceptual quality, especially on VoiceBank-DEMAND, using only five function evaluations.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Flow-matching speech enhancement method transferable to multilingual speech synthesis
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 流匹配语音增强方法可迁移至多语言语音合成
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 流匹配语音增强方法可迁移至多语言语音合成
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 流匹配语音增强方法可迁移至多语言语音合成
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: This paper proposes a skip-free encoder-decoder backbone with latent representation alignment for flow-matching speech enhancement, offering an efficient alternative that can inspire faster multilingual speech synthesis models.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 现有语音增强的扩散模型迭代采样慢。本文提出基于流匹配的无跳跃连接编解码器结构，通过潜在表示对齐指导生成，在少量函数评估下实现高质量语音增强。该方法的结构和效率优势可启发多语言语音合成模型的设计，加速合成过程。论文在语音增强任务上证明了有效性，但对合成领域有方法论迁移价值。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 现有语音增强的扩散模型迭代采样慢。本文提出基于流匹配的无跳跃连接编解码器结构，通过潜在表示对齐指导生成，在少量函数评估下实现高质量语音增强。该方法的结构和效率优势可启发多语言语音合成模型的设计，加速合成过程。论文在语音增强任务上证明了有效性，但对合成领域有方法论迁移价值。

quick_skim.title: Does Translation-Enhanced Speech Encoder Pre-training Affect Speech LLMs?
quick_skim.abstract: Connecting a pre-trained speech encoder to a Large Language Model (LLM) is the standard architecture for building Speech LLMs. However, a structural misalignment exists between the encoder and the LLM. Unlike encoders based on automatic speech recognition, which often produce representations in separate language-specific spaces, LLMs operate within a unified language-agnostic space. A mechanism is required to align the encoder's language-specific representations with the LLM's shared space. We argue that speech translation provides a principled way to achieve this. Unlike monolingual transcription, translation requires the model to bridge different languages and learn language-agnostic representations. We experimentally evaluate the impact of incorporating translation objectives into speech encoder pre-training. Our results demonstrate that translation-enhanced pre-training improves cross-modal integration and leads to superior performance across downstream Speech LLM tasks.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: speech translation pre-training for multilingual speech LLMs
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 语音翻译预训练用于多语言语音大模型

quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Investigates how speech translation pre-training aligns speech encoder with LLM for cross-lingual speech processing, benefiting multilingual TTS and ASR.

quick_skim.title: Adaptive Oscillatory Inductive Bias for Modeling Sharp Prosodic Dynamics in Diffusion-Based TTS
quick_skim.abstract: Diffusion-based text-to-speech (TTS) models have achieved significant improvements in speech quality. However, modeling sharp prosodic transitions and rapid pitch variations in expressive speech remains challenging. Existing diffusion-based TTS decoders commonly utilize periodic nonlinearities such as Snake activation function to capture harmonic structures, but this activation funcation provides limited adaptability when modeling abrupt amplitude and frequency variations. In this paper, we investigate the role of oscillatory inductive bias in diffusion-based TTS decoders and introduce an adaptive oscillatory nonlinearity that enables controllable periodic modulation while maintaining signal stability through a linear bypass component. We refer the resulting TTS system as OscillaTTS. Experiments on the LJSpeech and Emotional Speech Dataset show consistent improvements across objective and subjective evaluations, indicating improved modeling of expressive prosodic dynamics.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: improving prosodic dynamics in diffusion-based TTS
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 改进扩散式TTS中的韵律动态建模
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 改进扩散式TTS中的韵律动态建模
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 改进扩散式TTS中的韵律动态建模
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Introduces adaptive oscillatory nonlinearity for diffusion-based TTS decoders to model sharp prosodic transitions and pitch variations.

quick_skim.title: Temporal Validity in Retrieval Memory: Eliminating Stale-Fact Errors for AI Agents over Evolving Knowledge
quick_skim.abstract: Retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) gives agents access to accumulated knowledge, but has no model of time. When a fact changes (e.g., a function is renamed or API restructured), RAG retrieves both the stale and current value with near-identical embedding similarity. The agent then either abstains or serves the superseded fact. We show this is a structural problem: on a calibrated dataset, cosine similarity distinguishes a contradicted fact from a duplicated one with AUROC 0.59 (near chance), as contradictions are often more embedding-similar to the original than rephrased duplicates.   We present MemStrata, a retrieval memory maintaining temporal validity. It stores facts like RAG, preserving static recall, but when a fact's value is contradicted, a deterministic (subject, relation, object) supersession rule retires the stale value in a bi-temporal ledger - with no similarity threshold and no LLM call. Across six benchmarks run locally with a 7B model, MemStrata ties RAG on static knowledge and reaches 0.95-1.00 accuracy on evolving knowledge (where RAG reaches 0.20-0.47). The central result is the stale-fact-error rate: when required to answer, RAG serves superseded values 15-40% of the time; MemStrata drives this to ~0%, a failure class RAG cannot avoid. MemStrata achieves this at retrieval latency (~2.1s) versus ~16-18s for LLM-reranking baselines. We release the harness, datasets, and a marker-free evaluation protocol for memory under knowledge evolution.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: RAG method for temporal fact validity, relevant to factivity inference
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 用于时间事实有效性的RAG方法，与叙实性推理相关
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 用于时间事实有效性的RAG方法，与叙实性推理相关
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 用于时间事实有效性的RAG方法，与叙实性推理相关
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes MemStrata, a retrieval memory that maintains temporal validity of facts, addressing stale-fact errors in RAG for factuality tasks.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文指出检索增强生成缺乏时间模型，导致过时事实错误。提出MemStrata，一种维护时间有效性的检索记忆，能够在保留静态回忆的同时根据时间戳过滤过期事实。实验表明该方法有效消除了过时事实错误，对叙实性推理等任务具有借鉴意义。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 该论文指出检索增强生成缺乏时间模型，导致过时事实错误。提出MemStrata，一种维护时间有效性的检索记忆，能够在保留静态回忆的同时根据时间戳过滤过期事实。实验表明该方法有效消除了过时事实错误，对叙实性推理等任务具有借鉴意义。
